purpose: Primary grounding reference for Adlytic AI when analysing Meta Ads performance. Every recommendation should cite a metric definition, threshold, or decision rule from these modules rather than relying on general model knowledge.
usage_contract: Resolve a metric by key (e.g. 'ROAS', 'cost_per_message') across the metric modules.
Use `better_when` to read thresholds in the correct direction.
Prefer per-account relative baselines (Adlytic engine) over absolute benchmarks when a baseline exists; benchmarks are industry averages, not Meta guarantees.
Apply decision_rules.json for multi-signal verdicts; honour the cold-start guard before citing delta percentages.
Normalise currency to a common major-unit basis before comparing a stored value to a benchmark.

layers.engine_dropin: metaAdsKnowledgeBase.json — schema-conformant flat metrics[] loaded by loadKnowledgeBase.ts; actions returned verbatim on threshold breach. This is the file the runtime consumes.
layers.reasoning_reference: reference_core_metrics.json + metrics_*.json + benchmarks_by_industry.json + decision_rules.json — rich human/AI grounding layer (definitions, causes, multi-signal rules). Not loaded by the threshold engine; used to ground AI narration.

category_legend.cost: Per-unit cost metrics (CPC, CPM, CPA, CPL, CPI, cost_per_message, spend).
category_legend.delivery: Auction/delivery mechanics (frequency, reach, impressions, bid_strategy, learning_phase, placement, optimization_event).
category_legend.funnel: Conversion-funnel events (LPV, ViewContent, ATC, IC, purchase, abandonment).
category_legend.value: Business value (AOV, LTV, CAC, retention, ARPU family).

integrity_notes: Benchmarks are INDUSTRY AVERAGES from the cited 2026 sources, not official Meta standards.
ROAS is currency-factor-invariant and is never re-scaled in Adlytic currency repair.
cost_per_message must count exactly one canonical 'conversation started' event; never sum overlapping messaging action types.
